channel.title: Comments for page "How 173 Got to Site-19"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "How 173 Got to Site-19"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe I didn't catch the "Dr. Moto" reference when I first read this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is creepy as hell, largely because I share a first and last name with one of the characters named in this tale.

Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Answer 1: The Foundation department that handles alternate realities. Here's an introduction to them. That said, this is a rather radical interpretation of the Multi-U.

Answer 2: A character I made up for this Tale, and one of the few named Malay characters in this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's Multi-U? And who's Nurul Shafiqah binte Ahmad Ibrahim?
Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless Site-19 is in Memphis… but yeah, I believe I was inspired by SCP-2343's nickname.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, shouldn't it be: "How 173 Got to Memphis?"

lel

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

This doesn't really fit my headcanon

Same here, and I'm the author here. This is more of a secondary head-canon for me, since even I admit that it deviates too far from conventional Foundation (hence, me dubbing it a canon).

it's fairly dry for all that's occurring.

True, in my older revisions, there were more dynamic interaction scenes initially. By the end of everything, I swapped out almost every interaction with something impersonal:

  • A scene where 173 is being moved out of Site-91 the old fashion way –> an "updated" documentation of it
  • Dr. Long pleading with Agent Kisch in his office –> email exchange to demonstrate Foundation bureaucracy
  • An interaction among O5s about 2317 escaping and their more insidious actions –> The Foundation tab

And from 2317's page (Level 4 clearance),

Exploration of the alternate reality (SCP-2317-Prime) is limited

While we (the readers) know that 2317 is the Devourer entity, Foundation security hid that. I've based the terminology used in the transmission from the Level 4 version.

Does that mean that SCP-2317 has it's own timeline independent of all others

Maybe pocket dimension, or another previous ruined reality?

If so, does this mean the 2317 has permanently breached containment (broken the last chain), and if so, what's keeping it from destroying any other universe?

Well the Devourer is nigh-invincible, if the multiverse is truly infinite, even it will take quite some time before it does real damage. If they try to destroy the roots of the multiverse, that's another story…

Or does each universe have a door to its own 2317?

Maybe? Maybe not? But that's another story.

Please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IGNORE: I forgot I was replying to Petrograd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't really fit my headcanon, which is why I'm no-voting. The logic of shuffling around so many SCPs between universes is confusing to me unless you accept the central premise, and I'm not quite sold on that, making this more of a proposed-canon-infodump for me.

(Also because it uses Multi-U in a way that's somewhat insidious rather than Bailey-fueled fun, but again, totally a personal preference. Not a real criticism.)

Otherwise, I think it's well written, although it's fairly dry for all that's occurring. It's a bit tragic and blase to see people chatting blithely about layoffs when that's the only interaction we get with them before they're all killed off. (Also, the iPhone thing didn't bother me, because I figured there's no requirement that their 2045 CE matches our CE.)

I did have one question, though. So, it's stated that 2317 has escaped SCP-2317-Prime.
- Does that mean that SCP-2317 has it's own timeline independent of all others, and has just happened to escape into 5463-GOD?
- If so, does this mean the 2317 has permanently breached containment (broken the last chain), and if so, what's keeping it from destroying any other universe?
- Or does each universe have a door to its own 2317?
- Or is the 2317 documentation exclusively referring to a door from 5463-GOD?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit, I had to read the explanation in order to get this, but I think the connections to and uses of other skips are done well. This is probably the most interested 173-based anything to come out in a long time. Fixing one numbering issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for informing me that. In older revisions, it was Alex emailing to Bright… and I totally forgot about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the third collapsible, the e-mail to Dr. Moro is prefixed with "E-mail addressed to Dr. Bright" instead of Moto.
Besides that, I really like this, and would like to see a canon come out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mann's Proposal did not really occur to me while writing this, but I see where you're coming from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to think that this is the explanation for new skips spontaneously manifesting as mentioned in Mann's Proposal as well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for your compliments.

  1. I have to admit that "redoing 173's profile" was a last minute add-on after I thought about a number of failed Tales that tried something similar (present 173 via the SCP format, but "updated") and flopped.
  2. I will admit that 2317's use is questionable, although I wanted a end-of-the-world scenario (and I was scouting something from 2003 initially). I settled for 2317 since Clef mentioned on the KaktusKast that 2317's theme is based on Lovecraftian inevitability. I thought that it would be unique to play around that (Lovecraft never really thought of alternate realities anyway).
  3. Ditto. But I was inspired by my Message app, WhatsApp and similar apps made in China, Korea and Japan.
  4. Yup. Guilty as charged.
  5. Yeah… based on my time in this site, I've come to realise that if one desires to attempt a series/canon, each piece must hold out on its own first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You manage to do so many cool things with this tale. Breaking it down:

  1. You provide what is, in all honesty, a believable "origin" of sorts for 173 and where it comes from, as well as a "cleaner" version of 173's documentation and how it'd feasibly look if written today, without lessening the horror or impact of it.
  2. You manage to use 2317 in a way that, while certainly questionable, is kinda unique and justifies what Multi-U is doing.
  3. Your format here is awesome. Though I question how iPhone texting technology still looks the same 30 years into the future.
  4. You reference Alexandra, Nala, and Moto. Niiiice.
  5. If you really are planning on making this the start of something, it works perfectly as a standalone and gives me interest in what you're planning on doing.

This is one of the more refreshing tales I've read on the site in a while. Have a well-deserved +1.

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, I would to thank people who have looked through it such as Cimmerian, Wogglebug, Pheonix24 among others.

SCPs mentioned in this article are:

  • SCP-173 (The Sculpture) by Moto42
  • SCP-2317 (A Door to Another World) by DrClef

Stylistics for the iPhone-esque portion is unceremoniously adapted from (after some fiddling with the code to suit my purposes) LurkD's format of the AIAD series.

Naming scheme for universes are derived from Experiment Log T-98816-OC108/682, particularly the portion on SCP-507.

And WTH is happening here?

+ show block
– hide block

The true Foundation in this story operates on the multiversal scale and props up branches in various realities a la Scantron's proposal. SCPs are moved about across realities to extend time to study on them, and entire realities can be reduced to test sites. For instance, 2317 was unleashed just to observe it in the "wild".

And the "true" Foundation operates at the base of the multiverse, as a literal foundation of all reality.

Overall, this is 1/3 lampshading those "tales featuring 173 and the SCP format", 1/3 "why does 173's description begin with "Moved to Site-19…" and 1/3 the potential start to a series/canon?
